text:
Admission Date:  2011-10-12              Discharge Date:   2015-09-16

Date of Birth:  1999-09-01             Sex:   M

Service: CSURG

Allergies:
Cardura

Attending:Darrell
Chief Complaint:
72 year old white male with 1 year history of DOE which has
increased recently.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
MVR/CABGx2


History of Present Illness:
72 year old white male who had a 1 year history of DOE which
recently increased. He denies PND or orthopnea, and had a +ETT
and an echo which revealed inf. post. hypokinesis. He underwent
cardiac cath on 1984-11-03 which revealed: 100% proximal RCA
stenosis, 80% proximal LAD stenosis, 100% diagonal 1 stenosis,
and 90% OM1 lesion.  He had a 35% LVEF with 1-2+ MR.  Daniel Ellis is now
admitted for CABG.

Past Medical History:
H/O HTN
H/O Hypercholesterolemia
H/O diverticulitis
s/p T+A
H/O obesity

Social History:
Lives with his wife.
ETOH: none
Cigs: quit 25 yrs. ago

Family History:
unremarkable

bilaterally without bruits.

Abd: +BS, soft, nontender, without masses or hepatosplenomegaly

Brief Hospital Course:
The patient underwent CABGx2/MVR w/ #27 Mosaic tissue valve on
2011-10-12.  He had LIMA to the LAD and SVG to the OM.  Cross clamp
time was 100 minutes and total bypass time was 123 minutes.  He
had profound hypotension after he came off bypass and was
transferred to the CSRU on Levophed, Vasopressin, Amiodorone,
Insulin, Amicar, and Propofol.  He required large amounts of
volume and eventually the pressors were weaned off. He remained
intubated and on Epi, Vasopressin, Amio, and Lido on POD#1.
Milrinone was added as well.  He remained intubated and on these
drips until POD#4 when he was weaned off of Epi.
On POD#5 he was extubated and the milrinone was stopped.  He was
fairly disoriented, but slowly improved.  He had some sternal
intability, but a chest CT was unremarkable and there was not
drainage.  He was eventually oriented and was transferred to the
floor on POD#9. He grew out pseudomonas in his urine and was
treated with Ceftazadime and Cipro.  He had several bouts of
diarrhea, but had 3 negative c.diff cultures. He was transiently
treated with Flagyl and Vanco, but these were discontinued.  He
continued to progress and was discharged to rehab in stable
condition on POD#14.  He did have a moderate left effusion on
discharge, but it was improving with diuresis.

Medications on Admission:
Verapamil 240mg. PO in AM, 120 mg. PO in PM.
Lisinopril 40 mg. PO qd.
HCTZ 25 mg. PO qd
Atenolol 25 mg. PO qd
Tricor 160 mg. PO qd
ASA 325 mg. PO qd
Vitamin E

6. Atorvastatin Calcium 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO QD

7. Pantoprazole Sodium 40 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig:

Take all medications as prescribed
Call for any fever, redness or drainage of wounds

Followup Instructions:
